title: Chinese Teapots Face Challenges In Securing Crude Imports Despite Fresh Quotas
section_path: Chinese Teapots Face Challenges In Securing Crude Imports Despite Fresh Quotas
section_path_text: Chinese Teapots Face Challenges In Securing Crude Imports Despite Fresh Quotas
summary:
Main
This report outlines the latest crude flows and VLCC outlook, the progress of the SPR stockpile, and the impact of US tanker-specific sanctions on China. By Emma Li
China recently issued an additional crude oil import quota of over 6mt to some independent refiners for the remainder of 2024, mirroring a similar approach last year. Recipients are expected to use the allowance for seaborne cargos with customs clearance by December 31, or for barrels drawn from bonded storage tanks.
